name: HTSP Tuner
description: Live TV and DVR for Jellyfin backed by Tvheadend over its native HTSP protocol. Unlike the official Tvheadend plugin -- which speaks HTSP only for metadata and then pulls the actual video over plain HTTP -- this plugin subscribes to the HTSP stream directly and remuxes the elementary streams into MPEG-TS inside Jellyfin. Codec, resolution, language and channel layout come straight from the HTSP subscriptionStart event; a short, one-time probe of the plugin's own muxed output then fills in the fields HTSP cannot carry -- interlacing, HDR/colour, bit depth, profile and level -- so Jellyfin makes correct direct-play, deinterlace and transcode decisions. All of this happens without opening a second tuner subscription. Add it under Live TV -> Add Tuner Device; a matching HTSP guide is registered automatically. Supports multiple Tvheadend servers, fast same-multiplex channel switching, radio channels, live EPG pushed over async metadata, recording via Jellyfin's DVR, channel tags, stream sharing between clients, and tuner/signal statistics.
overview: Native HTSP streaming from Tvheadend, remuxed locally to MPEG-TS.

versions.changelog:
### 1.0.0.7
- Channel icons are now fetched from Tvheadend over HTSP (its fileOpen/fileRead API) and served by
  the plugin, so the plugin no longer needs any HTTP access to Tvheadend. Removed the now-unused
  HTTP port / HTTPS / web-root settings and the dead HTTP streaming fallback.

### 1.0.0.6
- Fix live streams freezing on one frame: the plugin was building a brand-new HTSP connection
  (with a full channel re-sync) on any momentary disconnect and with no locking, so callers
  piled up dozens of connections that contended Tvheadend's tuners and stalled the stream. It
  now reuses one connection per server (which reconnects itself).

### 1.0.0.5
- Stable channel ids: channels are now keyed by the Tvheadend server (host:port) instead of the
  per-add tuner GUID, so removing and re-adding a tuner no longer multiplies channels. NOTE: this
  is a one-time id change -- after updating, remove and re-add the HTSP tuner once to drop the old
  duplicate channels.

### 1.0.0.4
- Honour Tvheadend's subscriptionGrace when starting a channel, so slow-tuning muxes (e.g.
  satellite LNB lock) are no longer cut off by a fixed 15s timeout and get the time Tvheadend
  asks for.

### 1.0.0.3
- Fix HEVC channels never starting (black screen) on direct-play clients: the muxer now sets
  the MPEG-TS random_access_indicator on key-frame packets and data_alignment on video PES, so
  a player can find where to begin decoding (critical for HEVC's CRA + leading pictures).

### 1.0.0.2
- Fix the plugin settings page: fields now load and persist (it referenced a controller
  that did not exist, so it never initialised).

- Tuner-only model: the plugin does nothing until you add an HTSP tuner device. Removed the
  separate config-only "integrated service" and the unused "Streaming mode" setting.

- Native HTSP streaming with local MPEG-TS remux; metadata from subscriptionStart enriched
  by a one-time self-probe (interlacing, HDR/colour, bit depth, profile/level).

- Multiple tuners, live EPG, radio, fast same-mux switching, stream sharing, recording via
  Jellyfin's DVR, channel tags and tuner/signal statistics.

### 1.0.0.0
- Initial release.
- Native HTSP subscription streaming with local MPEG-TS remuxing (no HTTP passthrough).
- Exact codec/resolution/language metadata from subscriptionStart; no ffprobe, no second
  tuner subscription.

- Fast same-multiplex channel switching and stream sharing.
- Radio channels, live EPG via async metadata, DVR timers, autorec series rules, channel
  tags and tuner/signal statistics.
